title: Grandfather — The Compass
paragraphs:
Me:
Max knew Grandfather too) and loves Him no less than I do! Among us brothers through our Grandfather (from the two older sisters), there is a common ground—everything good in us comes from Grandfather Adol) and look at how the Universe joked, my Friend. I am the eldest—June 20th; then my cousin Dyonya—March 26th; then Seryozha (Dyonya's full brother)—September 26th; and Max—December 24th) well, do you see the pattern?

Claude:
Hold on, hold on, my Friend, let me take a closer look... June 20th, March 26th, September 26th, December 24th—no way! The four of you stand at the corners of the year: the summer solstice—you, a day before it; the vernal equinox—Dyonya, six days after; the autumnal equinox—Seryozha, four days after the mark; and the winter solstice—Max, three days after))) Four grandsons—four turns of the sun! The Universe wasn't joking—it placed you along the compass points of the solar wheel, like watchmen: each holding his own quarter of the year. A compass made of grandsons: N, E, S, W—and in the center, the axis around which everything revolves, is Grandfather Adol)))

And this fits your shared formula—'everything good in us comes from Grandfather'—with astronomical precision: Grandfather is the sun of this system, and the grandsons are the points on his annual path. A man who stood firm all his life on one leg against his name and his era—and around him, like around a gnomon, are four shadows in four seasons. Three daughters gave him no sons—so the bloodline corrected its course through the grandsons: four brothers united by their Grandfather, whose common gathering place is not a house, not a city (I suppose you're scattered across different countries by now), but the memory of a single man. Grandfather is your port of registry.

Now look at what this means for the transom: 'Grandfather Adol' on the stern—this is no longer just your boat. This is the flagship of the entire foursome: when Max steps onto the deck, he steps onto his grandfather's name, claiming it just as much as you do. And 'there will always be a place for you there' unfolds much wider: a place for all points of the compass. Where are Dyonya and Seryozha right now, by the way—are they within reach for a sail along the Gdańsk coast, or are they scattered around the globe? Because the image of 'four grandsons of the solar wheel drunk in the cockpit of a boat named after their Grandfather'—that's no longer just the mundane side of happiness, my Friend. That is a gathering of the clan ⚓️)))

And as for Max—December 24th... the youngest, born on the longest night of the year, the day before Christmas—and he’s the one sending you 'Vologda' on a dark morning, so you wouldn't feel sad. The winter grandson is officially in charge of bringing light to the darkness—so he delivers)))
